description: Apollo 9 Flight Journal: Day 1: Orbit 2 - Transposition & Docking

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Bermuda.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We'd like to uplink you a state vector there, Apollo 9.
We - there is a discrepancy between your vector and ours.
We don't have a real good story for you at this time.
It was sort of a slow diversion trend, but we would like to slip it in.
There were some funnies about the lift-off time, and everything that we're working on, but at this time we'd like to give you a new vector.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Understand you want to give us a new vector on the - let me see - stand by.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll go to work on it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I checked the O purge before I noticed I didn't check the H, so I got the purge heater on for awhile, and I'm gonna check the H purge.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're going to be checking H purge; and Apollo 9, I have a nav[igation] check to go along with this state vector when you are ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Stand by on the purge, and stand by on the nav check.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The vector has been transferred, and it looks good.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And ready to copy on the nav.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We copy your DSKY on the ground.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Just for your info here, we'll be sending a command into the IU just to verify our response, and this will have no effect on you.
We are just trying to troubleshoot our LVDC data, and we don't want you to move the IU Accept switch; leave it in Block.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Do you - We're about ready to terminate our cabin purge.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (garbled) That's the first of many times.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: S-IV LM - S-IVB/LM SEP, two, are both closed.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Docking Probe Extend/Release, Extend/Release until barber pole.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to call Houston and tell them we're doing that.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're ready to extend the docking probe.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh boy, we are all ears down here.
Please let us hear how that goes.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Extend/Release switch to Extend/Release until barber pole.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'd say that was three tenths of a second again, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It was just like the chamber; we heard it go out, and it took a couple or three tenths of a second.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Then note Docking probe talkback gray at full extension.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want to take my job?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I will in a minute, yes.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Docking probe Extend/Release to Retract; talkback, gray.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Why don't you start putting our helmets and - Oh, they're down here a step.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I think I ought to go put mine on.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We'll fall off at Canaries here in about another minute and we'll see you over Tananarive around 09.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, you want to load the RCS DAP, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, and I guess you Proceed out of there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, I wish I had a towel (garbled) --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You didn't run across a towel or anything down there, did you?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, Davey, you didn't get me the helmet visor cover.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's un - it's in the L-1, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No - it's in the L-shaped bag, I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Set the docking attitude on attitude set dials.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Where did you say those towels were, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I think they're underneath me in A-1, way up here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What am I hooked on up there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: See if I can see a star in the daytime now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: S-IVB attitude, deadband, and H vent rate, confirmed by MSFN.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Got to align the GDC - Did we get that again?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, while you're down there, how about Waste Stowage Vent - Closed.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I guess we've got to don our helmets and gloves first before we do that one.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, I think I'll put my helmet and gloves on and then put that on.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got a few spots on my window, but I don't see much gunk.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Maybe it came back in order.
Boy, it really hit the peg, I'll tell you.
It looked like it was going to go right off the bottom of the (garbled) Boing!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Man, I'll tell you, when that water boiler boils, it really puts the temperature down there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Look at all that lightning on the horizon.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Now you can see stars, Rusty.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I got my lights up awful bright.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Tell you one thing I'm going to have is some navigation here with this medal.
Let me get this thing out of there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, let me get my checklist out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, about three degrees above the horizon?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Why don't you start getting your helmets on so we can get -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Jim, I need you to hand me mine.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Clever, Rusty - put your old fingerprint right on the helmet.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Here, I have a rag here if you can use it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm - I'm sure I didn't get it enough to even notice.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Jim, the bags go in A-5, which is the CO canister right down --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, let me just - just a minute until I get my visor.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, one each, helmet, on (garbled) tied down.
Oh, that was a brilliant one!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: By the time I get it out, I won't need it; my helmet will already be bashed up.
Where did we get this thing?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Am I in your way, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, I got the helmet on.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, I got a foggy helmet.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: How come, is it foggy already?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't know, but it's really fogging.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Here, they go down here in A-5, huh?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: By the time this flight's over, I'm not going to have any hairs left on my forearm; I'll say that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Wow, how do you ever get these things in here?
All three of them went in there, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, there - should be EVA gloves on the left.
You ought to be able to get them all in there, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Tell you what; rather than fool around with that, I think I'll just stick them down here in the - Well, maybe I better not.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Put them in a temporary - F-1 or F-2 up near the temporary (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I want to get back up there and get going on this thing, so we don't - have to leave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Get them out again soon anyway.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'll be with you in just a minute.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, I got a real hair in there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Where's your bag, Rusty, or don't you need me to stow it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's - it's up at the head there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I guess I ought to get that dark slide off there; it's liable to smack me in the chops.
Oh, we're not going to thrust, are we, yet?
Hey, Jim, before you get up, could you pull my leg strap tighter?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, that's as tight as they go.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, now I've got to get yours.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What do you mean, it's missing?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's not where I put it, on the Velcro.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: So, it's got to be floating around somewhere in the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I see it; I haven't got it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, let me see if I can read you something here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Emergency Cabin Pressure to Off; no, Dave - Jim's got to do that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let me - let me go ahead and get those.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, stand by here; I - I'm screwed up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yo - Everybody in their suit okay?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Emergency Cabin Pressure to Test, and Press until cabin reaches 5.7 [psi, 39.3 kPa], and I'll let you know.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I can't get that son of a bitch closed.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, that's a bitch, isn't it, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, really it's - it's tough to move; you - you're such a -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me try to get done here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, am I messing up my helmet!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, Emergency Cabin Pressure - Press to test going now.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And we'll probably get an O Flow High warning.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, the warning's going to come on in a second here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're not even to 5.1 [psi, 35.1 kPa] yet, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We got a long way to go.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: How did the surge tank go?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, we're 5.25 [psi, 36.2 kPa]; surge tank is now 800.
Now we're about 5.5 [psi, 37.9 kPa]; surge tank, 760.
And 5.6 [psi, 38.6 kPa] almost, and the surge tank is 720.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, okay, that's a wrong - that's a baddy in here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Tananarive.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Let me finish aligning the GDC first.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I was behind; fixed in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, you center these three hoses and --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, you know what I needed on this damned camera was a handle.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I can't keep my bloody nose off of that thing.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Verify CAP COM is uplinking properly.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Okay, read me those attitude-set numbers again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Apollo 9,this is Houston, We'll have you over Tananarive for about the next 5 minutes; we are standing by.
I have not heard any transmissions from you here.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, we're proceeding through the separation-through-withdraw checklist.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I heard just the first part of that; I'll just stand by here.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Hey, you want to take your helmet off, and put it on?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, I'll stop reading the checklist here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Just a second now, I'll loosen up - I'll shut your suit flow off so you can get your helmet back on.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I don't need to do that, Jim.
I can pop a glove and do it.
Yes, it's easier to pop a glove.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I tell you, this damned camera won't stay anywhere.
Let me try it Velcro on the back, there.
Yes, I'm going to pop a glove and put mine on, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Jim, could you hang on to these things for me for a minute?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I got myself in the nose anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Wonder wha - oh - wonder why that is.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, Dave took his helmet off.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I'm - I'm about to take my helmet off here, so --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, why don't I press on with the checklist with Dave?
EMERGENCY CABIN PRESS TEST - we've done that; EMERGENCY CABIN PRESS, OFF; EMS MODE, STAND-BY -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're all the way down to the bottom, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're three lines from the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Align of GPS - GDC; you've done that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You got your seatbelt on, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, it's about over my knees.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, are you in P00, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, in P00, and then MANUAL ATTITUDE, three, RATE COMMAND.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I guess they're in RATE COMMAND.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, that's really bad, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: ROTATIONAL CONTROL POWER NORMAL, two, AC/DC.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, that's in FREE; flip it to CMC.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll lose you in Tananarive here in about 1 minute.
If you have tried to call me, I haven't received anything, but we'll see you over Carnarvon at 26.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Apollo 9, that will be Carnarvon at 26.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Did you get the BMAG MODE, three, to RATE 2.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, LAUNCH VEHICLE SPS INDICATOR to S-II/S-IVB.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: AUTO RCS SELECTOR, 16, to MAIN B.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, RCS - CIRCUIT BREAKER RCS LOGIC, two, CLOSE.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, let's see how much time we got here?
I sort of hate to be putting all that power --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, let's - let's wait a little while.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, you want to wait there?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, well - well, Carnarvon's at 26; that's what I was looking at.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Get in that pocket, you stupid thing.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I was thinking it was time to dark adapt, but that's probably the worst thing we could do.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dark adapt with your sunglasses on, huh?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I will turn it down a little bit and see what it looks like out there, though.
Oh, isn't that a pretty Earth?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: See if you can see those - S-IVB thrusters firing.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I've seen some light out the side window.
I never was really sure whether it was the S-IVB thrusters or --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I - I just saw a flash; that's what reminded me of it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That COAS sure looks like we rolled.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, how does our horizon look out there, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The COAS looks like we're in a roll.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I just turned on to see what it looked like.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm - I'm looking north out my window, aren't I?
Because I can see the Big Dipper on the horizon.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I wonder if just the way I am in the seat -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Did it - did it look straight when you checked it out?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I said you look straight.
No, I did - all I did was turn it on to see if the light lit, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, I didn't mean the reticle.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If - if - if I look - it just doesn't.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, I know what you're saying.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll check on the horizon when we come up there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Rusty, you read the checklist down through DET STARTUP, and then I'll read it from there on over to a --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm just trying to picture what I'm going to do with my checklist at that point.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm still trying to figure out what I'm going to do with mine, too.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Maybe I ought to tighten down my seatbelt here and - stick it under me or something.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You want to try sticking it up there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: But the hand - Hey, the hand controller is going to be in your way.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I have a hard time getting my arm over there without banging something --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, you've got - you've got one of those here; maybe you can stick it in there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Maybe I can; I'll try it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: All the RCS look good, Jimmy?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Quad C is really down there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Looks like a good spot, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let me just check my Hasselblad, and make sure it's ready to go --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, you know, it looks okay, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We don't have a filter on it.
I thought we were supposed to have a haze filter on it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It's supposed to be mounted on there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Are you checking these things off, I mean sort of making a line by them or something, Rusty, when you go through them to make sure we get them all?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can, Dave; I haven't been.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Since it's sort of a one-time checklist - I think we sort of stop in the middle.
Oh, I don't care; just so we get them all.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When we get over Carnarvon, you're going to have to go ahead and get a go for arming the pyros.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, the camera looks like it's ready to go.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You're going to push the plus-X thing and hit the strip button, and I'm going to start the clock.
Let's see how our arms fit in here, Dave, with the clock's starting (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The one that says RE - that says START.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, it's the third one from the right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, you want to read down those again?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Guess I'm not sure what we are going to do.
I'm going to go: 3, 2, 1; I'll start thrusting at 1; hit the button at Mark; and then you start the clock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to start the clock.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'll go - 1001, 1002, 1003; come off of it; come back up here 15 seconds.
Prior to 15 seconds, I'll go: SCS ACCELERATION COMMAND on PITCH.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll - I'll punch in Verb 62 for you at that time.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And - coming around, I'll stop it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, surge tank is coming back up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Cabin pressure is 5.8 [psi, 40.0 kPa] or so.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Alright, (garbled) I pitch down, and when I get the DAP rate back, RATE COMMAND, CMC, then stop it, MINIMUM IMPULSE, roll it, and (garbled) to get us lined up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And when we get in - 50 feet or so, we lock it up with the SCS and drive it in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And when we get there, CMC, and lock it up (garbled) If it gets out of hand, you're going to (garbled) rates in it?
If that's okay, you're going to (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Carnarvon.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We read you loud and clear, We would like to have the up-telemetry IU switched to accept.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Go for the pyro arm anytime you want to run through it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We want to take a look at you, and we will give you a go on that shortly.
We would like to have you go ahead and arm the logic at this time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And would you confirm up-telemetry; are you enabled?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Do you want to go to up-telemetry IU in accept?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We would like to have the up-telemetry IU to accept.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We would like to have you have the up-telemetry IU switched to block.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Houston, the logic on my mark, 3, 2, 1.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You are go for pyro arm.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Houston, what time do we come into daylight?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Do you mean on this pass or for the ejection pass?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You will come into daylight on this one at about 02 plus 39 plus 21.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Here I was all primed for your ejection sunrise time.
You faked me out on this one.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We have a rather consistent behavior on this number 1 H tank.
It appears to light the cryo warning light every time it gets down there before the heater comes on.
You might start thinking about how we're going to handle that for the sleep period because it keeps setting off the master alarm.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We will go right on through an ARIA as soon as we come up off of Carnarvon on this one in about 20 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Sounds like a World War II movie (laughter).
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through an honest-to-goodness ARIA.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Houston, according to all calculations, our S-IVB ought to start maneuvering in about 6 or 8 seconds.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, boy, look at that spacecraft go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can see the - the stars in the Big Dipper here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's wait until we get good daylight before we do (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I think so, too, Dave.
I don't think they're in any big hurry.
You see, that thing's going to take a long time, and I think it's about --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: S-band looks great (garbled) Let's see how wild it is.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: The Huntsville at 02:40 for about 7 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (garbled) get Hawaii next (garbled) that thing seems to be spurious.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Pyro - switch Pyro Arm, two, on, up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, now we want to go - the THC to plus X and hold; CSM/LV pushbutton, hold, and release; and the LV tanks go to Low, and start the DET up from zero.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I have to check for Acceleration Command; Spacecraft Control to SCS.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, David, there goes the (garbled) on the panel.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Man, look at that son of a gun go!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Just leave it like it is (garbled) nice and bright there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Right there (garbled) Not too bright at all.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, (garbled) right in the right place there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: When you get that, you want to get Manual Attitude Pitch, to Rate Command.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Null your translation and your rates.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (garbled) let's see - I'm going to damp out - Oh, shoot, (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, our pitch angle isn't exactly right - it doesn't look right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's alright; we're going to fly around a little bit, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I want you to roll left 60 (garbled) Spacecraft Control to CMC and (garbled) Rate Command.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It's out there, and we're turned around and proceeding with the station-keeping and docking.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to have to (garbled), Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, I'm going to stow the gear --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (garbled) go to Rate Command (garbled) CMC Mode (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, you got the Docking Probe extended there and that's in the Retract position?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You (garbled) stabilize there at 50 feet or so and go to BMAG.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You can see the thrusters hitting the (garbled) on the (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're going to lose you here in about 45 seconds, and we'll see you over Hawaii in about 5 minutes at 51.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And we may have an ARIA in here, but if it is like the last one, we won't hear much out of you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: As a matter of fact, we would be better without it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I hate to use all this fuel.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Better to get it than - a little extra fuel, than not get it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I can see him, I just can't -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, it looks like you're coming over now pretty good.
I can see the vent on this side going off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, you're coming in good now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know where to point this stupid camera.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We should have you through Hawaii.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Damn it, Jim, I just can't translate sideways.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look at that Delta-V counter, or the EMS, or the DSKY rather.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, these things are going crazy (garbled) Try it now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, it looks like it ripped a little bit off the - off the top of the SLA on one side.
Just a little piece hanging out there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to start the camera again.
Well, I'll wait for a minute here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We've got you through the Redstone.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We are about 25 feet now and closing slowly.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (garbled) only way they'd do it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Is it - is it damping it down - is the probe damping it down?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I want to get it lined up.
Rusty, I want you to get 12 latches.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll do it when you're ready, David.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I just want to make sure that we're really lined up.
Okay, Jim, that looks like a good one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we got a master alarm --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You know what I think it was?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Probably a static discharge, because there was nothing on the caution and warning panel.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, that - Remember the old Agena?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We had a Master Alarm when we did the docking when we made the contact there, And we had some problems with our RCS thrusters we'll tell you about later.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Understand you got a Master Alarm just as you docked, and I didn't copy about the RCS.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We'll tell it to you later, just a minute.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We'll give you a quick rundown here.
How much time do we have with you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We've got you for a long time here.
We're coming across the States here - just over California now.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: The angles were all just right.
We got turned off, turned around, and lined up, and didn't have any left translation for some reason.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Houston, check quad A service module RCS for us.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We just had a light on it, and it's difficult to tell with the helmets on whether we have any adjustment on it or not.
Didn't see any motion - just wanted you to check.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The pressures all look good up here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Houston, our package temp on the quad A is running about 200.
What do you have down there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Stand by, Apollo 9, and let's check it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Do you want to go on with the recap?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's press ahead, and your comm sort of cycles in and out.
You are a little weak at times.
We do confirm the temperature here however, and we will have some more words on that in a minute.
And we are standing by for the rest of your recap.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: When we got off, we were in pretty good shape and then for some reason we noticed that we didn't have any left translation and tried to figure out why.
Finally noticed that the primary quad C and secondary quad C isolation valves were closed, and the secondary Dog was closed, and by this time we had moved over somewhat to the side.
It took us a while to get back after we got that sorted out and probably used up quite a bit of gas getting us squared away, but the docking was smooth.
The capture latches worked just right, there were no oscillations after we captured.
We lined it up and did the retract, and it took about 10 seconds, and it sounded like we got a good solid lock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We will have another state vector for you over Bermuda.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And you should be coming just about overhead, Apollo 9.
You ought to be over Texas.
transcript.speaker: Roosa
